Title: Tropane alkaloid biosynthesis: a century old story

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Increase precursor supply for hyoscyamine/scopolamine production by overexpression in TA-producing Solanaceae or heterologous hosts; combine with PMT/MPO/PYKS/CYP82M3/H6H stacking. Monitor polyamine homeostasis and growth effects.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes tropane alkaloid accumulation by supplying putrescine precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene identification; overexpression and knockdown/RNAi in A. belladonna; metabolite analysis; GFP localization; prior inhibitor/enzyme activity studies
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-associated; cytosolic protein observed in tobacco protoplast after AbODC-GFP expression
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: In TA-producing roots; EcODC highest in buds and rolled leaves of E. coca
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcript/gene cloning reported; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Role in TA production was long inferred from inhibitor and tracer studies; direct genetic evidence reported only recently in A. belladonna. Relative importance versus ADC appears species-dependent.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Perturbation may affect polyamine metabolism, plant growth and stress responses.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Potential flux-limiting in some species/root cultures; not universally established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbODC catalyzes ornithine decarboxylation to putrescine for TA biosynthesis. 2. Knockdown and overexpression in A. belladonna significantly altered TA levels. 3. Acts as an upstream precursor-supply enzyme whose contribution can be species-dependent relative to ADC.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots and alkaloid-accumulating tissues of Solanaceae; not specified in detail in this review
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense-related plant specialized metabolites; medicinal anticholinergic compounds

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Can be used to boost precursor putrescine supply in engineered cocaine pathway reconstruction, but must be balanced with ADC and polyamine metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: cDNA cloning and heterologous expression; expression-metabolite correlation in young leaves
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest in buds and rolled leaves, matching active cocaine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: cDNA library from young E. coca leaves; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review emphasizes expression correlation rather than direct knockout evidence in E. coca.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcODC was cloned and shown enzymatically active. 2. Transcript abundance peaks in tissues with highest cocaine biosynthesis. 3. Functions in upstream precursor supply.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Atropa belladonna; Datura candida × Datura aurea
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for enhancing putrescine formation through the arginine route, especially in species where ADC contributes strongly; combine with ODC and downstream pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Decarboxylates arginine to agmatine, feeding putrescine formation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports TA biosynthesis by contributing to putrescine precursor pool.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme assays; inhibitor studies; cDNA cloning/heterologous expression in E. coca; metabolic labeling evidence
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: In E. coca highest in buds and rolled leaves; in oat chloroplast localized ADC cited as reference
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Same as applied species where cloned
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: cDNA library from young E. coca leaves; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Importance for TA production is species-dependent; in some cultures ADC and ODC both may be flux limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May perturb polyamine metabolism and stress responses.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Potentially flux-limiting in some root cultures; not universally established
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Not reported in TA plants reviewed; chloroplast localization cited for oat ADC
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ADC contributes an alternative entry from arginine into TA biosynthesis via putrescine. 2. EcADC was cloned and shown active. 3. Relative contribution versus ODC depends on species and tissue.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots for Solanaceae TAs; buds/rolled leaves for cocaine

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species: Belladonna; Henbane; Tobacco; Datura; Hyoscyamus muticus; Scopolia parviflora
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Atropa belladonna; Hyoscyamus niger; Nicotiana tabacum; Datura stramonium; Hyoscyamus muticus; Scopolia parviflora
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A prime engineering target as the first pathway-specific committed enzyme; overexpression can elevate hyoscyamine/scopolamine in transformed roots and is effective in gene-stacking strategies. Must consider species-specific response and precursor/cofactor supply.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers a methyl group from S-adenosyl-L-methionine to putrescine, forming N-methylputrescine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances pathway flux into tropane alkaloids; overexpression increases hyoscyamine and/or scopolamine in multiple systems.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme activity assays; cDNA cloning; expression analysis; overexpression in transformed roots; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Committed step enzyme / supplies dedicated pathway intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Root-specific, notably pericycle cells in A. belladonna
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Roots of TA-producing Solanaceae; tobacco roots
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: cDNA library screening; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evolved from spermidine synthases; mutagenesis studies support evolutionary transition from SDPS/SPDS enzymes.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May divert putrescine from polyamine metabolism; species-dependent increase in products.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SAM-dependent methyltransferase; evolved from spermidine synthase family
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Established as first specific/committed enzyme in TA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PMT is the first committed and pathway-specific enzyme to tropane alkaloids. 2. Root-specific expression and transgenic overexpression elevate TA production. 3. It channels putrescine into N-methylputrescine and away from general polyamine metabolism.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Good target for increasing TA production in hairy roots and synthetic biology platforms; stack with PMT and downstream enzymes. Need to manage H2O2/ammonia byproducts and amine metabolism balance.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidatively deaminates N-methylputrescine to 4-methylaminobutanal, which cyclizes to N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes hyoscyamine and scopolamine accumulation by forming the immediate pyrrolinium precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Biochemical purification/activity assays; gene cloning; root-specific expression analysis; RNAi in hairy roots; metabolite analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: H. niger roots; tobacco roots; A. belladonna roots
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to copper amine oxidase family; only AbMPO1, not AbMPO2, matches canonical TA-pathway expression and function.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changes may affect diamine metabolism and reactive oxygen species/ammonia production.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MPO oxidatively deaminates N-methylputrescine to the aldehyde precursor of N-methylpyrrolinium. 2. AbMPO1 RNAi significantly reduced hyoscyamine and scopolamine. 3. AbMPO1 is the functional TA-pathway MPO in A. belladonna.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Atropa belladonna; Anisodus acutangulus; Datura stramonium
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A key engineering target to reconstruct tropane core formation in heterologous hosts. Combine with PMT, MPO, CYP82M3, TRI and downstream enzymes. Watch non-enzymatic side products such as cuscohygrine.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses malonyl-CoA-derived units to generate 4-(1-methyl-2-pyrrolidinyl)-3-oxobutanoate, either directly from N-methyl-Δ1-pyrrolinium plus two malonyl-CoA equivalents or via acetonedicarboxylate formation followed by nonenzymatic Mannich condensation, depending on study interpretation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for formation of the tropane core precursor; silencing reduces tropinone and all downstream TAs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics-guided identification; VIGS; recombinant enzyme assays; LC-MS product detection; crystal structure/intermediate trapping
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mechanistic interpretation differs between reports: direct formation of 31 versus production of acetonedicarboxylate 14 followed by nonenzymatic condensation with N-methylpyrrolinium.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Flux redirection may create side products (e.g., cuscohygrine) and require balancing malonyl-CoA supply.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for tropane core formation; bottleneck potential in pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Latin_Name: Atropa belladonna; Anisodus acutangulus; Datura stramonium
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PYKS is an atypical type III PKS essential for constructing the C3 unit leading to the tropane core precursor 31. 2. Silencing lowers 31, tropinone and downstream TAs. 3. Structural work suggests possible formation of acetonedicarboxylate followed by spontaneous condensation.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential gene for reconstructing the complete tropane core pathway; co-expression with PMT/MPO/PYKS enhances tropinone formation in transient or microbial systems.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for tropinone formation and all downstream tropane alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptomics-guided discovery; transient coexpression in N. benthamiana; in vitro microsome assay; VIGS and metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome comparison of lateral roots vs non-root tissues; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as a cytochrome P450 catalyzing a noncanonical ring-forming oxidation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: None directly reported; may accumulate unstable precursor 31 if limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for tropinone-forming cyclization
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbCYP82M3 is the tropinone synthase converting 31 to tropinone. 2. VIGS reduced tropinone and all downstream TAs while increasing precursor 31. 3. Enzyme activity was confirmed in microsomes and transient expression assays.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Hyoscyamine/scopolamine branch of tropane alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Favor TRI to direct flux to medicinal 3α-hydroxytropane alkaloids (tropine-derived branch); useful in pathway balancing against TRII. Important for engineering product spectrum.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Stereospecific NADPH-dependent reduction of tropinone to tropine (3α-tropanol).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directs tropinone flux toward tropine-derived alkaloids such as hyoscyamine and scopolamine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme purification; cDNA cloning; recombinant expression; inhibitor studies; crystal structures; mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly in lateral roots; cell-specific in endodermis and outer cortical layer
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: cDNA library screening; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reversible in HnTRI; stereospecificity determined by substrate-binding orientation and active-site electrostatics.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering TRI/TRII balance changes entire TA spectrum, potentially reducing pseudotropine/calystegine-related metabolites.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRI stereospecifically reduces tropinone to tropine. 2. TRI and TRII define the metabolic split between tropine- and pseudotropine-derived alkaloids. 3. Structural and mutational studies explained opposite stereospecificity relative to TRII.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Pseudotropine/calystegine branch of tropane alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Manipulate TRII to redirect flux to pseudotropine-type alkaloids or calystegines; suppression can favor tropine-derived medicinal alkaloids.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Stereospecific NADPH-dependent reduction of tropinone to pseudotropine (3β-tropanol).
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Diverts tropinone flux away from hyoscyamine/scopolamine toward pseudotropine-derived products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Enzyme purification; cDNA cloning; recombinant expression; feeding and inhibitor studies; crystal structures; mutagenesis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly in lateral roots; pericycle, endodermis and inner cortical layers
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: cDNA library screening; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Reaction essentially irreversible in HnTRII; pivotal determinant of product spectrum.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing TRII may lower hyoscyamine/scopolamine by diverting shared precursor tropinone.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TRII stereospecifically reduces tropinone to pseudotropine. 2. TRI/TRII ratio controls tropane alkaloid spectrum. 3. Structural studies defined determinants of opposite stereochemistry compared with TRI.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Roots/rhizomes and other tissues depending on species

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Required for cocaine pathway reconstruction; distinguishes Erythroxylaceae branch from Solanaceae TRI/TRII system. Useful for selective reduction to methylecgonine in synthetic biology.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Stereospecific reversible reduction of methylecgonone to methylecgonine with 3β-configuration.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides the immediate alcohol precursor for cocaine esterification.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purification of enzyme activity from young leaves; gene cloning; biochemical characterization; expression pattern analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest activity, protein and transcript levels in young expanding leaves
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Belongs to aldo-keto reductase family, not TRI/TRII SDR family; also reduces several related tropanones.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Broader substrate range may create off-pathway alcohols.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MecgoR, not TRI/TRII, reduces methylecgonone to methylecgonine in cocaine biosynthesis. 2. Expression peaks in young leaves where cocaine is synthesized. 3. The enzyme belongs to the aldo-keto reductase family and shows broader substrate tolerance.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tropic acid/phenyllactate branch of hyoscyamine-scopolamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Increase aromatic acyl moiety supply for hyoscyamine/scopolamine by overexpression, especially when tropine accumulates; best used with PPAR, UGT1 and LS.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers an amino group between L-phenylalanine and 4-hydroxyphenylpyruvate to generate phenylpyruvate and L-tyrosine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for phenyllactate and downstream hyoscyamine/scopolamine formation; silencing reduces these alkaloids and increases tropine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Differential transcriptomics; VIGS; recombinant enzyme assay; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: de novo transcriptome from multiple tissues; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Connects primary aromatic amino acid metabolism to specialized tropate formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ab-ArAT4 catalyzes the first step toward the tropate/phenyllactate moiety from phenylalanine. 2. Silencing lowered phenyllactate, hyoscyamine and scopolamine while raising tropine. 3. It links aromatic amino acid metabolism to TA acyl-moiety biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Tropic acid/phenyllactate branch of hyoscyamine-scopolamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important for increasing phenyllactate supply; stack with ArAT4, UGT1 and LS to improve hyoscyamine/scopolamine production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes production of phenyllactic acid and downstream hyoscyamine, anisodamine and scopolamine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome mining; tissue expression; in vitro enzyme assay; RNAi root cultures; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Secondary root-specific; accumulates in root pericycle and endodermis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome data mining; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as an HPPR homologue with HAD domain.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Hydroxyl acid dehydrogenase-domain reductase / HPPR homologue
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AbPPAR reduces phenylpyruvate to phenyllactate for tropate branch biosynthesis. 2. RNAi reduced phenyllactate, hyoscyamine, anisodamine and scopolamine. 3. Expression is strongly root-specific in pericycle/endodermis.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Littorine precursor branch of hyoscyamine-scopolamine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful to improve littorine formation via activated phenyllactate donor supply; stack with PPAR and LS.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers glucose from UDP-glucose to phenyllactic acid to form phenyllactylglucose.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for littorine, hyoscyamine and scopolamine production by generating the activated acyl donor for LS.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome candidate screening; VIGS/RNAi; in vitro enzyme assay; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Required for storage/modification of target product precursor
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome database search; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Supports a glucose-ester mediated acyl-donor mechanism rather than phenyllactoyl-CoA in littorine biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altered glycosylation may affect related hydroxyacid pools.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGT1 generates phenyllactylglucose as the activated donor for littorine formation. 2. Knockdown reduced littorine, hyoscyamine and scopolamine. 3. Discovery established a glucosylated intermediate route in TA acylation chemistry.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Atropa belladonna; Saccharomyces cerevisiae; Nicotiana tabacum
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Critical target for reconstructing medicinal TA production; should be co-engineered with trafficking/vacuolar targeting modules in non-plant hosts because localization and processing strongly affect activity.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers the phenyllactoyl moiety from phenyllactylglucose to tropine to form littorine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for littorine, hyoscyamine and scopolamine accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome candidate screening; VIGS/RNAi; transient expression in tobacco; yeast engineering and localization studies; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Heterologous-host trafficking context strongly affects activity
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptome database search; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: SCPL-AT requiring correct vacuolar localization/posttranslational processing; in yeast, N-terminal fusion aids functional expression.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mislocalization in heterologous systems causes loss of function; engineering may require additional trafficking components.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point in heterologous pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. LS forms littorine from tropine and phenyllactylglucose. 2. Knockdown sharply lowers littorine, hyoscyamine and scopolamine while causing precursor accumulation. 3. Proper vacuolar targeting and proteolytic processing are crucial for activity in heterologous hosts.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Hyoscyamus niger; Nicotiana tabacum; Hyoscyamus muticus
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential enzyme to convert littorine into the rearranged tropate scaffold; useful in reconstitution platforms, but should be paired with HDH because product is hyoscyamine aldehyde.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes oxidative rearrangement of littorine to hyoscyamine aldehyde.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for conversion of littorine into hyoscyamine pathway intermediate; suppression decreases hyoscyamine and accumulates littorine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Subtracted cDNA library screening; VIGS in H. muticus; heterologous expression in tobacco hairy roots and yeast; product identification
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: P450 inhibitor clotrimazole blocks this conversion
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Subtracted cDNA library; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Defines a new CYP80 subfamily member involved in unusual carbon skeletal rearrangement via radical chemistry.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Accumulation of littorine/intermediates if downstream reduction is limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for unusual rearrangement step
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CYP80F1 catalyzes the oxidative rearrangement of littorine to hyoscyamine aldehyde. 2. Silencing lowers hyoscyamine and causes littorine accumulation. 3. The reaction explains the long-enigmatic carbon skeleton rearrangement in tropate formation.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Atropa belladonna; Datura innoxia; Datura stramonium; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Mandatory for complete pathway reconstruction from littorine to hyoscyamine; can be selected from orthologues with higher activity (e.g., DsHDH) in microbial engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reduces hyoscyamine aldehyde to hyoscyamine using NADPH.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes formation of hyoscyamine from littorine-derived aldehyde intermediate.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression analysis; activity verification in yeast de novo pathway platform; mutagenesis and modeling
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Coexpressed with TA pathway genes in A. belladonna roots
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: A. belladonna, D. innoxia and D. stramonium orthologues identified
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified in 2020 as the last missing enzyme in the hyoscyamine/scopolamine pathway; zinc-dependent MDR enzyme.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No direct tradeoff reported; choice of orthologue affects pathway productivity in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Zinc-dependent alcohol dehydrogenase; medium-chain dehydrogenase/reductase superfamily
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; important completion step in pathway reconstruction
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HDH is the long-missing enzyme reducing hyoscyamine aldehyde to hyoscyamine. 2. Identification enabled full reconstruction of the hyoscyamine/scopolamine pathway. 3. DsHDH showed the highest activity among tested orthologues for yeast engineering.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and medicinal alkaloid precursor to scopolamine

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Hyoscyamus niger; Atropa belladonna; Nicotiana tabacum; Escherichia coli
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Scopolamine branch of tropane alkaloid biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Premier target for increasing medicinal scopolamine over hyoscyamine; overexpression efficiently converts hyoscyamine/6β-hydroxyhyoscyamine to scopolamine in plants and microbes. Best stacked with upstream pathway genes.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Bifunctional 2-oxoglutarate/Fe2+-dependent dioxygenase that hydroxylates hyoscyamine at C-6β to anisodamine and further epoxidizes 6β-hydroxyhyoscyamine to scopolamine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Drives conversion of hyoscyamine into anisodamine and scopolamine; a decisive step for scopolamine production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Partial and homogeneous purification; cDNA cloning; heterologous overexpression in plants and E. coli; isotope labeling and mechanistic studies
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in scopolamine-producing cultures and roots
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: cDNA library cloning; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Bifunctional hydroxylase/epoxidase; excludes 6,7-dehydrohyoscyamine as obligatory intermediate in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increased scopolamine may reduce hyoscyamine pools; substrate promiscuity may generate oxidized side products.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. H6H hydroxylates hyoscyamine to anisodamine and epoxidizes anisodamine to scopolamine. 2. Overexpression efficiently boosts scopolamine formation across hosts. 3. This bifunctional terminal enzyme is decisive for final product identity.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Defense and important anticholinergic medicinal alkaloid

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential for final-step cocaine production in pathway reconstruction; substrate preference could be leveraged to make cocaine analogs, though application is constrained by controlled-substance considerations.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers benzoyl or cinnamoyl group from acyl-CoA thioester to methylecgonine to form cocaine or cinnamoylcocaine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Directly forms cocaine, the terminal product.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transcriptome mining; recombinant protein assays with methylecgonine and benzoyl-CoA/cinnamoyl-CoA; activity comparison among BAHD candidates
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified from E. coca transcriptome; tissue specificity not detailed here beyond pathway context
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: E. coca transcriptome; no accession provided
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: EcBAHD7 is far more efficient than EcBAHD8 for cocaine/cinnamoylcocaine formation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Can also form cinnamoylcocaine depending on acyl-CoA supply.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. EcCS is the BAHD acyltransferase catalyzing the final benzoylation to cocaine. 2. EcBAHD7 is much more active than EcBAHD8 and was designated cocaine synthase. 3. Acyl-CoA substrate supply can shift product profile toward cocaine or cinnamoylcocaine.
